question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a Juju charm can observe, grouped by category:

---

## ⚙️ Lifecycle Hooks
| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `install` | Once, before any other hook, during unit provisioning |
| `start` | When unit initialisation is complete |
| `config-changed` | When config is updated or IP addresses change; also fires after `install` and `upgrade-charm` |
| `update-status` | Periodically at a regular interval (default: every 5 minutes) |
| `upgrade-charm` | After the charm code itself is updated |
| `stop` | When unit destruction is being prepared |
| `remove` | Final hook before the unit is removed |

---

## 👑 Leadership Hooks
| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `leader-elected` | When this unit is elected as the application leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | When leadership changes or the leader modifies leadership settings |

---

## 🔗 Relation Hooks
*(where `<endpoint>` is the relation endpoint name defined in `metadata.yaml`)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | When a relation is first established between two applications |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | When a new remote unit joins an existing relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | When a related unit's relation data is updated |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | When a remote unit leaves the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | When the entire relation is terminated |

---

## 🔒 Secret Hooks
| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `secret-changed` | When a secret this unit consumes has a new revision available |
| `secret-rotate` | When a secret this unit owns needs rotating (rotation period elapsed) |
| `secret-expired` | When a secret revision this unit owns has reached its expiry time |
| `secret-remove` | When an old secret revision can be safely removed |

---

## 💾 Storage Hooks
*(where `<storage>` is the storage name defined in `metadata.yaml`)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | When a storage volume becomes available to the unit |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | When a storage volume is about to be removed |

---

## 🐳 Workload / Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes charms)
*(where `<container>` is the container name defined in `metadata.yaml`)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | When the Pebble daemon in the container is ready |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | When a Pebble custom notice is emitted by the workload |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | When a Pebble health check transitions to failing |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | When a previously failed Pebble health check passes again |

---

## 🖥️ Series Upgrade Hooks *(Juju 3.6 and earlier only)*
| Hook | When it fires |
|------|---------------|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Before an OS series upgrade begins |
| `post-series-upgrade` | After an OS series upgrade completes |

---

**Total: ~25 distinct hook types** (plus many instances for each named relation, storage, and container). The relation, storage, and workload hooks are all prefixed with the relevant name from your `metadata.yaml`, so the actual number of hook entry points in a given charm scales with its configuration.
